SUSTAINABILITY Our World FAQS BASE RUM BACARDÍ Carta Blanca COCKTAILS Daiquiri (shaken) Discover how to make the perfect Daiquiri cocktail at home! The Original BACARDÍ Daiquiri recipe combines BACARDÍ Carta Blanca rum with fresh lime juice and sugar for a crisp, refreshing drink. Learn the simple steps to create this iconic rum cocktail! view all cocktails view all cocktails WHAT YOU WILL NEED Daiquiri (shaken) Ingredients 50 ml BACARDÍ Carta Blanca 25 ml fresh lime juice 2 tsp caster sugar Cubed & crushed ice Coupette glass Servings SHOP BACARDÍ Copy Daiquiri (shaken) ingredients Copied! Daiquiri Recipe One step at a time Step 1 Stir Add the fresh lime juice and sugar to your cocktail shaker, then stir until the sugar completely dissolves. Step 2 Pour Pour in BACARDÍ Carta Blanca rum, then fill the shaker with half cubed ice and half crushed ice. Step 3 Shake Shake vigorously until the shaker becomes frosted and ice-cold to the touch. Step 4 Serve Strain through a fine strainer into a chilled cocktail glass for the perfect Daiquiri. daiquiri Tasting notes What does a Daiquiri taste like? This classic rum cocktail is famous for its perfect balance of flavours. Created using BACARDÍ Carta Blanca rum, fresh lime juice, and sugar, the traditional Daiquiri recipe has long been considered the true test of a skilled bartender. tips Mix like a pro Chill your glass! A cold glass makes a world of difference for a perfectly refreshing drink. To achieve this, either keep your glass in the freezer or fill it with ice and water while you prepare your cocktail. Empty it just before serving for the ultimate chill factor. HOW DO YOU MAKE A FROZEN STRAWBERRY DAIQUIRI COCKTAIL? To make a frozen strawberry Daiquiri, simply add 6 strawberries to your frozen Daiquiri recipe. Opt for Caster Sugar! Caster sugar (or superfine sugar) is ideal for a hand-shaken Daiquiri. Its fine texture dissolves easily with lime juice, adding balanced sweetness and a subtle, sherbet-like note for a bright, zesty mouthfeel. Juice Your Lime in Advance! Juicing your lime a few hours before making your cocktail can soften its acidity, mellowing the sharpness and creating a smoother mouthfeel in the final drink. where it all began ENGINEERING THE PERFECT DRINK The Daiquiri was born in the Cuban town of Daiquirí, where American engineer Jennings Cox mixed lime, sugar, and BACARDÍ rum to beat the tropical heat. It later rose to fame at Havana’s El Floridita bar, championed by bartender Constantino Ribalaigua Vert. With fans like Ernest Hemingway, it became a global icon—crisp, refreshing, and effortlessly elegant. bacardí cocktails the daiquiri family From strawberry Daiquiri to frozen variations, explore our rum cocktails based on the classic Daiquiri!
